text: I’ve confirmed the migration surface: server rendering, ISR, dynamic SSG fallback, custom document metadata, shared layout, and API handlers. I’m reading the supporting components and styles now so the new `app/` structure preserves behavior instead of only moving files.

output.output:
components/AppProvider.js
styles/globals.css

text:
Plan:
1. Create typed shared utilities and client wrappers so App Router pages can stay mostly server-rendered while preserving navigation actions.
2. Build `app/layout.tsx`, route pages, `not-found.tsx`, and `app/api/.../route.ts` using App Router metadata, params, headers, ISR, and `notFound()`.
3. Remove `pages/`, then run a build to catch type or API mismatches and fix anything the App Router rejects.

I’m starting the file edits now. The main changes are converting shared layout and context into typed App Router components, moving data fetching to server components with `headers()`/`fetch` caching options, and rewriting API routes as `route.ts` handlers with `NextResponse`.

The App Router files are in place and the Pages Router files are gone. I’m running a build now to let Next.js validate the route handler signatures, async `params` shape, metadata exports, and any App Router-only constraints before I clean up the now-empty `pages/` tree.

input.command: /bin/bash -lc 'nmem --json m search "workspace migrated from Next.js Pages Router to App Router; pages directory removed"'

/bin/bash -lc 'nmem --json m add "Migrated the /home/user/workspace Next.js app fully from Pages Router to App Router on July 18, 2026. Replaced pages routes with app/ server components, replaced pages/api with app/api route handlers using NextResponse, moved shared shell into app/layout.tsx, converted shared components to typed TypeScript, added not-found and error boundaries, and removed the pages directory entirely." -t "Next.js App Router migration completed" --unit-type decision -l nextjs -l app-router -l migration -s codex -i 0.87'

text:
Migrated the app fully to the Next.js App Router and removed `pages/` entirely.

The shared shell from `_app` and `_document` now lives in [app/layout.tsx](/home/user/workspace/app/layout.tsx), with typed client providers in [app/providers.tsx](/home/user/workspace/app/providers.tsx) and [components/AppProvider.tsx](/home/user/workspace/components/AppProvider.tsx). Route pages were moved to [app/page.tsx](/home/user/workspace/app/page.tsx), [app/blog/page.tsx](/home/user/workspace/app/blog/page.tsx), and [app/blog/[id]/page.tsx](/home/user/workspace/app/blog/[id]/page.tsx). I replaced Pages Router APIs with App Router patterns: `getServerSideProps` became a dynamic server component using `headers()` and `fetch(..., { cache: "no-store" })`, `getStaticProps`/ISR became `revalidate` plus server `fetch`, and `getStaticPaths` became `generateStaticParams`. Dynamic metadata now uses `generateMetadata`, 404 handling is [app/not-found.tsx](/home/user/workspace/app/not-found.tsx), and error handling is [app/error.tsx](/home/user/workspace/app/error.tsx).

API routes were migrated from `pages/api` to typed route handlers at [app/api/posts/route.ts](/home/user/workspace/app/api/posts/route.ts) and [app/api/posts/[id]/route.ts](/home/user/workspace/app/api/posts/[id]/route.ts), using `NextResponse` and App Router route signatures. I also added typed shared data helpers in [lib/posts.ts](/home/user/workspace/lib/posts.ts) and a small client navigation component in [components/BackButton.tsx](/home/user/workspace/components/BackButton.tsx).

Verification: `npm run build` passed successfully on July 18, 2026. Next.js also updated [tsconfig.json](/home/user/workspace/tsconfig.json) to include `.next/dev/types/**/*.ts` during the build.
